refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication packet loss at or below the customer-defined threshold (acceptance criterion: ≤1% under agreed network conditions) to ensure safe and effective remote equipment operation.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-009 addresses a critical quality-of-service requirement for teleoperation. Packet loss directly impacts operator situational awareness, control responsiveness, and safety. Refining the FR to explicitly reference the acceptance criterion (≤1%) and network conditions clarifies the measurable boundary and reduces ambiguity in verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct network stress testing under representative worksite conditions (latency, interference, bandwidth constraints) with packet loss measurement via packet capture and telemetry analysis. Validate that packet loss remains ≤1% across the agreed operational envelope.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss events in real-time.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error correction or retransmission mechanisms to minimize packet loss impact on control and telemetry.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall adapt communication parameters (e.g., compression, frequency) based on network conditions to maintain packet loss ≤1%.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation system shall provide operator feedback when packet loss approaches or exceeds acceptable thresholds.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement redundant communication channels (dual network paths) with automatic failover to reduce single-point-of-failure packet loss.
DP-B: Deploy adaptive codec and compression algorithms that adjust payload size and transmission rate based on real-time network quality metrics.
DP-C: Integrate forward error correction (FEC) and selective retransmission protocols at the application layer to recover lost packets without excessive latency.
DP-D: Establish a dedicated, quality-of-service (QoS) managed network link with prioritization of teleoperation traffic over other worksite data flows.
DP-E: Combine DP-B, DP-C, and DP-D: adaptive compression, FEC/retransmission, and QoS-managed network to achieve multi-layered packet loss mitigation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-E: Implement a multi-layered packet loss mitigation strategy combining adaptive codec/compression (DP-B), forward error correction with selective retransmission (DP-C), and QoS-managed network link prioritization (DP-D). This approach addresses packet loss at multiple layers (application, transport, network) and provides graceful degradation under adverse conditions.

potential_sources: Coupling between adaptive codec logic and network quality monitoring (both depend on real-time telemetry).
Coupling between FEC/retransmission and QoS management (both affect bandwidth utilization and latency).
Coupling between operator feedback (FR-NGHE-009.4) and adaptive parameter adjustment (FR-NGHE-009.3) – both require shared network state visibility.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via a centralized network quality monitoring module that feeds metrics to both adaptive codec and FEC/retransmission subsystems. Decoupling is achieved through well-defined interfaces (e.g., NetworkQualityMetrics interface). No fundamental design contradiction identified.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-defined packet loss threshold and network conditions are formally documented and stable.
Assumption: Worksite network infrastructure supports QoS tagging and prioritization.
Risk: Adaptive codec may introduce latency jitter if compression/decompression is CPU-intensive; mitigation: hardware acceleration or edge processing.
Risk: FEC overhead increases bandwidth consumption; mitigation: adaptive FEC strength based on observed loss rate.
Risk: Retransmission may exceed acceptable control loop latency; mitigation: selective retransmission only for critical telemetry, not real-time control.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the acceptable latency budget for retransmission and adaptive parameter adjustment. If control loop latency must be <100ms, retransmission strategy must be constrained to non-critical telemetry only. Recommend adding a sub-FR for latency bounds.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: The Teleoperation module owns the end-to-end teleoperation communication stack, including adaptive codec/compression, FEC/retransmission logic, network quality monitoring, and operator feedback mechanisms. It interfaces with the Network Link for packet transmission and with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation packets (control commands, telemetry, status). Teleoperation module sends control/telemetry packets and receives acknowledgments and network quality metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound telemetry stream from Vehicle (sensor data, equipment state). Teleoperation module consumes telemetry, applies compression/FEC, and transmits to tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outbound control commands from tele-operator to Vehicle. Teleoperation module receives operator input, applies error correction, and transmits via Network Link.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety-critical alerts and status. Teleoperation module receives SafetyAlert from Safety Services and may trigger operator feedback or communication parameter adjustments in response to safety events.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning DP-E to NGHE Solution would diffuse packet loss mitigation logic across multiple sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), violating cohesion. Not recommended as primary owner.

Vehicle module is responsible for equipment control and onboard telemetry. Packet loss mitigation (FEC, adaptive codec, QoS) is not a vehicle-level concern; it belongs in the communication layer. Assigning here would diffuse communication logic into vehicle firmware, reducing reusability and clarity.

Teleoperation module is the natural owner of teleoperation communication quality. It encapsulates adaptive codec, FEC/retransmission, network quality monitoring, and operator feedback. High cohesion with DP-E. Minimal diffusion risk.

Safety Services owns safety-critical logic and alerts. While packet loss impacts safety, the mitigation mechanism (FEC, adaptive codec) is not a safety function per se; it is a communication quality mechanism. Safety Services should consume SafetyAlert from Teleoperation, not own the packet loss mitigation logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal owner of DP-E (multi-layered packet loss mitigation). It has high functional cohesion with adaptive codec, FEC/retransmission, and network quality monitoring. External interfaces (Network Link, Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and minimal. Ownership is clear and reusable across different vehicle types and safety policies.
containment_risks: Risk: Teleoperation module may become complex if adaptive codec and FEC logic are not cleanly separated into sub-components. Mitigation: Define internal sub-modules (AdaptiveCodec, FECEngine, NetworkQualityMonitor) with clear interfaces.
Risk: Network Link interface may not provide sufficient granularity for QoS tagging and priority marking. Mitigation: Extend Network Link interface specification to include QoS metadata.

Risk: SafetyAlert feedback loop may introduce latency if Teleoperation waits for Safety Services acknowledgment before adjusting communication parameters. Mitigation: Use asynchronous SafetyAlert subscription; Teleoperation reacts independently to network quality metrics.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the internal architecture of Teleoperation module: define sub-components for AdaptiveCodec, FECEngine, and NetworkQualityMonitor with explicit internal interfaces. Extend Network Link interface to include QoS metadata (priority, latency budget). Document the interaction between SafetyAlert and adaptive parameter adjustment to avoid circular dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-E (multi-layered packet loss mitigation) directly addresses the refined FR (maintain packet loss ≤1%). The combination of adaptive codec, FEC/retransmission, and QoS management provides multiple mechanisms to achieve the acceptance criterion. No fundamental mismatch between problem (packet loss) and solution (layered mitigation).

diffusion_risks: Adaptive codec logic could diffuse into Vehicle module if not clearly owned by Teleoperation. Mitigation: Explicit ownership assignment and interface contracts.
FEC/retransmission could be duplicated across multiple communication paths if not centralized. Mitigation: Single FECEngine sub-component within Teleoperation.

Network quality monitoring could be scattered across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: Centralized NetworkQualityMonitor within Teleoperation, published via Telemetry interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module provides strong containment for DP-E. All packet loss mitigation logic is localized. External interfaces are minimal and well-defined. No significant diffusion risk if ownership boundaries are enforced.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of packet loss ≤1% is feasible via network stress testing and packet capture analysis. Teleoperation module can expose network quality metrics (packet loss rate, latency, jitter) via Telemetry interface for monitoring and validation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of DP-E to Teleoperation module is clear. Ownership, interfaces, and sub-component responsibilities are well-defined. No ambiguity in who owns what.

recommended_revisions: Formalize the Network Link interface specification to include QoS metadata (priority, latency budget, packet loss target).
Define acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (FR-NGHE-009.1 through 009.4) with measurable verification methods.
Document the latency budget for adaptive parameter adjustment and retransmission to ensure control loop stability.
Specify the interaction protocol between Teleoperation and Safety Services for SafetyAlert feedback.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-structured and decomposed into four atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-009.1 through 009.4). DP-E is a sound multi-layered solution with manageable couplings. Teleoperation module is the optimal owner with high containment and low diffusion risk. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to define specific design parameters for adaptive codec, FEC/retransmission, and network quality monitoring. Proceeding to the next level of functional decomposition will clarify implementation details and reduce design risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: The teleoperation system shall detect and report packet loss events in real-time.
FR-NGHE-009.2: The teleoperation system shall implement error correction or retransmission mechanisms to minimize packet loss impact on control and telemetry.
FR-NGHE-009.3: The teleoperation system shall adapt communication parameters (e.g., compression, frequency) based on network conditions to maintain packet loss ≤1%.
FR-NGHE-009.4: The teleoperation system shall provide operator feedback when packet loss approaches or exceeds acceptable thresholds.

assumptions: Customer-defined packet loss threshold (≤1%) and network conditions are formally documented and stable.
Worksite network infrastructure supports QoS tagging and prioritization.
Teleoperation module has access to real-time network quality metrics (packet loss rate, latency, jitter).
Control loop latency budget is compatible with selective retransmission (assumed <100ms for critical control, <500ms for telemetry).
Safety Services provides SafetyAlert interface for asynchronous safety event notification.
open_risks: Adaptive codec may introduce latency jitter under high CPU load; requires hardware acceleration or edge processing validation.
FEC overhead increases bandwidth consumption; requires tuning of FEC strength based on observed loss rate.
Retransmission may exceed acceptable control loop latency; requires selective retransmission strategy for non-critical telemetry only.
Network Link interface may not provide sufficient granularity for QoS tagging; requires interface specification extension.
SafetyAlert feedback loop may introduce circular dependencies or latency; requires asynchronous design validation.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of customer-defined packet loss threshold and network conditions (worksite topology, interference sources, bandwidth constraints).
Network stress test results demonstrating packet loss ≤1% under agreed conditions with DP-E implementation.
Latency measurements for adaptive codec, FEC/retransmission, and operator feedback mechanisms.
Bandwidth utilization analysis for FEC overhead and adaptive compression.
Control loop stability analysis with selective retransmission strategy.
Interface specification for Network Link, Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert with QoS metadata.
Design review of Teleoperation module internal architecture (AdaptiveCodec, FECEngine, NetworkQualityMonitor sub-components).
